title: Text Parser — Command Processing
description: Add a text parser that handles multi-word player commands like 'go north', 'pick up sword', or 'use health potion'. Use string methods like find() and substr() to split the command into a verb and an object. For example, 'pick up sword' should extract verb='pick up' and object='sword'. Handle at least 4 different commands (go, look, pick up/take, use). Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'string' in cpp, 'Use the string type for command processing'
assert 'find' in cpp or 'find(' in cpp, 'Use string::find() to locate spaces or substrings in commands'
assert 'substr' in cpp or 'substr(' in cpp, 'Use string::substr() to extract verb and object from commands'
assert 'parseCommand' in cpp or 'parse_command' in cpp or 'processCommand' in cpp or 'process_command' in cpp, 'Create a function to parse or process player commands'
import re
command_count = sum(1 for cmd in ['go', 'look', 'pick', 'take', 'use', 'get', 'examine'] if cmd in cpp.lower())
assert command_count >= 3, f'Handle at least 3 different command verbs like go, look, pick up, use (found {command_count})'
acceptance_criteria: Uses string::find() to locate spaces in player commands
Uses string::substr() to extract the verb and object
Handles multi-word commands like 'pick up sword'
At least 3-4 command verbs are recognized (go, look, pick/take, use)
Invalid commands show a helpful error message
Commands are processed through a parser function
hints: size_t spacePos = command.find(' '); to find the first space
string verb = command.substr(0, spacePos); string object = command.substr(spacePos + 1);
Check for 'pick up' specially: if (verb == "pick" && object starts with "up ") then re-parse
Use string::npos to check if find() didn't find a space: if (spacePos == string::npos)
Consider converting to lowercase for comparison using tolower()
